content: A black hole is a place in space where gravity is super strong, much stronger than anything we feel on Earth. This happens because a big star has collapsed into a tiny, super heavy ball that pulls everything close to it. When something, like light or a spaceship, gets too close to a black hole, it can’t escape because the gravity pulls it in really hard. Light normally moves super fast, but the black hole’s gravity is so strong that even light can’t run away from it. Inside a black hole is a place called the singularity, where all the star’s matter is squeezed into a tiny point with no size but huge weight. Anything that falls into a black hole gets crushed by the strong gravity and can’t come out again, like being stuck forever. Even though we can’t see black holes directly, scientists know they are there because they see how stars and gas move around them. Black holes can slowly shrink and disappear over a very, very long time, but that takes much longer than we can imagine.
